items.title: Deep Learning
items.description: State-of-the-art Gen AI relies on deep neural networks (transformers, U-Nets, etc.) for representation and generation. Deep Learning explains the stack; this topic explains the generative capability layer.

items.title: What is Generative AI?
items.description: Discriminative vs generative framing, joint vs conditional distributions, application domains, and history live in the Gen AI Fundamentals stage—linked here so this taxonomy page stays concise.

items.title: Types of Generative Models
items.description: Autoregressive, VAE, GAN, and diffusion families are model mechanisms—not repeated here. Use that topic after you know where Gen AI sits in the stack.

items.title: Self-Supervised Learning
items.description: Large generative models are frequently pretrained via self-supervision (next-token prediction, masking, denoising). That learning paradigm is canonical under types of learning, not under this taxonomy label.

items.title: Large Language Models (LLMs)
items.description: LLMs are the dominant text Gen AI systems today. Scaling, tokenization, and instruction behavior are covered in llms-fundamentals rather than duplicated on this placement page.

items.title: Machine Learning
items.description: Gen AI is a branch of ML defined by generative outputs. Supervised classifiers and regressors share the same parent but optimize different objectives and deliver different products.
